artPrompt: An evocative scene in a dim back room where a goblin Don leans forward across a card table, steepling his clawed fingers with a knowing smile, a thick bulging unmarked file at his elbow as a sweating figure squirms in the shadows opposite. A single amber lamp isolates the confrontation, cigar smoke hanging. Mono-black noir of ink shadow and smoky brown, the weight of incriminating knowledge, composed wide in a 4:3 landscape frame, rendered as a vibrant gouache storybook illustration — saturated matte color, simplified flat-ish shapes, soft painterly edges, decorative.
name: The Don Knows What You Did
text:
The player to your left must confess, out loud, the worst Magic play they've made this game. The whole table votes on whether it was really that bad. If the table votes yes, that player owes you a Favor (create a Favor token) and loses 2 life out of shame.
If nobody confesses within ten seconds — count it on your fingers, slowly, menacingly — every opponent owes you a Favor instead. The silence said enough.
flavorText: He keeps a file on everyone. The file is mostly just "yes."
